intake.summary: Gulf Horizon Properties (fictional) wants to tokenize part of a UAE commercial real estate portfolio and let investors buy fractional ownership, with onboarding, income distribution, and integration into existing property systems.
primary_goals: Tokenize a first tranche of buildings for fractional investment
functional_requirements: Issue tokens representing fractional building ownership
non_functional_requirements: Keep sensitive data within the UAE where possible
Must connect to: Yardi Voyager, Microsoft 365, Salesforce CRM
constraints: Must integrate with existing Yardi property-management system
Investor data should stay within UAE where possible
Board wants a clear view of regulatory exposure before approving
open_questions: Which specific buildings are in the first tranche?
Are target investors retail, accredited, or institutional?
What is the licensing position for offering tokenized assets?
What does the Yardi integration need to read and write?

compliance.disclaimer: These are review-required flags for a qualified human, not legal advice. Nothing here is a final compliance conclusion.

review_areas.why_it_matters: Offering fractional ownership tokens to investors may fall under virtual-asset or securities oversight.
review_areas.who_should_review: UAE-licensed legal counsel and a virtual-asset compliance specialist

review_areas.why_it_matters: Onboarding and secondary transfers need identity verification and anti-money-laundering checks.
The client asked to keep investor data within the UAE where possible.
review_areas.who_should_review: Data protection lead with UAE PDPL knowledge

review_areas.why_it_matters: International investors may trigger rules in their own jurisdictions.

data_handling_notes: Confirm where investor KYC documents are stored and who can access them.
Map which token actions are recorded on-chain vs off-chain for privacy.

recommended_services.reason: Core need: represent fractional building ownership as tokens.
Encode ownership, transfers, and income distribution safely.
Give non-crypto-native investors a simple way to hold tokens.
Ground an investor-support assistant in verified documents only.

proposed_architecture: Token contract on an audited Layer 1/Layer 2 representing fractional ownership
Custodial wallet so investors need no crypto knowledge
Automated rental-income distribution via smart contract
Integration layer syncing ownership with the property-management system
Human-reviewed investor-support assistant grounded in approved documents

effort_and_risk_flags.note: Smart-contract audits and KYC integration are hard to rush.
Token offering may need licensing before any public pilot.
Two-way sync of ownership records can be complex.

phasing: Phase 1: Compliance review, architecture sign-off, single-building proof of concept
Phase 2: KYC onboarding, audited contracts, custodial wallets, income distribution
Phase 3: Secondary transfers, multi-building rollout, full system integration

proposal.title: RWA Tokenization Discovery - Gulf Horizon Properties
proposal.executive_summary: Gulf Horizon Properties wants to tokenize a first tranche of UAE commercial buildings for fractional investment within one quarter. Elchai can deliver this with audited smart contracts, KYC-gated onboarding, custodial wallets, and integration into existing property systems. The biggest dependency is regulatory review, which must clear before any public pilot.
scope_outline: Audited smart contracts for ownership and income distribution
out_of_scope: Multi-building rollout before the proof of concept is approved
suggested_next_steps: Confirm regulatory position with UAE-licensed counsel
Agree the single building for the proof of concept
proposal.human_review_note: Verify all compliance flags and set pricing before sending to the client.
